methodology_sections: 1.1 Asset Inventory - Financial assets, state assets, privilege assets, reputation assets
1.3 System Architecture Mapping - Contract hierarchies, dependencies, upgrade patterns, access control
2.1 Entry Point Enumeration - Public/external functions, callbacks, parameter analysis

financial_assets: ERC20 prizeToken balances held by PrizePool (prize liquidity, reserve, unaccounted contributions)
ERC20 underlying asset balances held by each Vault (transiently during deposit/withdraw and possibly idle balances)
ERC4626 yieldVault shares/assets held by each Vault (principal backing; source of yield)
Vault ERC20 share token supply (represents depositor principal claims; also minted during liquidation/yield fee minting)
Claim fees accrued in PrizePool as claimerRewards (withdrawable prizeToken)
Yield fee accrual in Vault as _yieldFeeTotalSupply (claimable as newly minted Vault shares)
Any ERC20 approvals set by Vault: (1) underlying asset approval to yieldVault (max), (2) underlying asset approval to liquidationPair (max)
state_assets: TieredLiquidityDistributor: _tiers mapping (tier drawId, prizeSize, prizeTokenPerShare snapshots)
PrizePool: vaultAccumulator + totalAccumulator ring buffer observations and metadata (contribution smoothing accounting)
PrizePool: draw lifecycle state (_winningRandomNumber, claimCount, canaryClaimCount, largestTierClaimed, _lastClosedDrawStartedAt, _lastClosedDrawAwardedAt, _totalWithdrawn)
Vault: TWAB balances and totalSupply stored in TwabController (authoritative ledger for Vault share balances)
TwabController: userObservations/totalSupplyObservations ring buffers and details, delegates mapping
privilege_assets: PrizePool drawManager authority (closeDraw, withdrawReserve; and de-facto control of reserve/prize distribution cadence)
PrizePool drawManager initialization race surface (setDrawManager callable by anyone until set)
Vault owner authority (Ownable): setClaimer, setLiquidationPair, setYieldFeePercentage, setYieldFeeRecipient, and inherited ownership transfer functions
LiquidationPair privileged caller for Vault.liquidate (only liquidation pair may mint shares for yield liquidation)
Vault claimer authority (address allowed to batch-claim prizes via Vault.claimPrizes)
User-controlled hook configuration in Vault (_hooks mapping), enabling arbitrary external calls during prize claim flow
TwabController has no explicit access control on mint/burn/transfer/sponsor beyond using msg.sender as vault key; integrity relies on Vault not exposing those calls to arbitrary external actors
reputation_assets: Fairness/correctness of winner selection (TWAB history correctness, tier odds, vault portion, winning random number integrity)
Protocol solvency perception: Vault collateralization invariant (exchangeRate >= assetUnit) and PrizePool accountedBalance invariant vs real token balances
Governance-like influence via delegation/sponsorship in TwabController (affects chance-weighting / eligibility)
Claimer auction pricing credibility (VRGDA fee computation and max fee enforcement relative to prize size)

system_architecture.inheritance_hierarchy: PrizePool inherits TieredLiquidityDistributor. Vault inherits OpenZeppelin ERC4626 and ERC20Permit, implements ILiquidationSource, and inherits owner-manager-contracts/Ownable. Claimer inherits OpenZeppelin Multicall. TwabController is standalone but depends on TwabLib/ObservationLib ring-buffer logic. Libraries: TwabLib uses ObservationLib + RingBufferLib; DrawAccumulatorLib uses RingBufferLib and PRBMath SD59x18; TieredLiquidityDistributor/PrizePool use PRBMath types and TierCalculationLib; Vault uses OpenZeppelin SafeERC20/Math and external yieldVault (IERC4626) + PrizePool + TwabController.
external_dependencies: OpenZeppelin: IERC20, SafeERC20, ERC4626, ERC20, ERC20Permit (draft), Math, Multicall
Solmate: SignedWadMath (wadExp, wadLn, wadMul, wadDiv, unsafeWadMul, etc.)
v5-liquidator: LiquidationPair and ILiquidationSource interface package
forge-std/console2.sol (debug import in PrizePool; should be removed/guarded for production builds)
External ERC4626 yield vaults (IERC4626) integrated by Vault
External PrizePool used by Vault and Claimer
External TwabController used by Vault and PrizePool
system_architecture.upgrade_patterns: No proxy/diamond upgrade patterns detected in provided contracts. All contracts are deployed via constructors (immutable configuration prevalent). VaultFactory deploys new Vault instances permissionlessly.
system_architecture.access_control_structure: PrizePool: onlyDrawManager modifier gates closeDraw and withdrawReserve; drawManager is settable once via setDrawManager (no auth, front-runnable until set). Vault: onlyOwner gates administrative setters (setClaimer, setLiquidationPair, setYieldFeePercentage, setYieldFeeRecipient); liquidation() gated to msg.sender == _liquidationPair; claimPrizes() gated to msg.sender == _claimer; hooks are user-settable. TwabController: no explicit roles; write functions are callable by anyone but affect balances under the key of msg.sender vault address (integrity depends on vault contract being the only one calling for itself).

state_changes: TieredLiquidityDistributor._tiers (canary and possibly new tiers reset)
ERC20 permit allowance on underlying asset (external token state)
ERC20 permit allowance on underlying asset (external token state)
TwabController delegates mapping for _receiver (if not already sponsorship)
ERC20 permit allowance on underlying asset (external token state)
ERC20 allowance for shares if _caller!=_owner (inherited allowance state in ERC20)
External hook contracts may mutate arbitrary state (user-chosen)
ERC20 underlying asset allowance to liquidation pair (external token state)
totalSupplyObservations[msg.sender] (delegateBalance adjustments depending on sponsorship transitions)
delegate balances and possibly total supply delegateBalance
External: PrizePool claim state (via Vault.claimPrizes -> PrizePool.claimPrize)

upgradeability_surface.notes: Abstract-like base with internal mutators; used via inheritance by PrizePool. No proxy storage concerns.
Constructor-initialized with multiple immutables. Critical one-time setDrawManager is not access-controlled until set.

Constructor initializes immutables and approves yieldVault max allowance. Inherits Ownable; ensure inherited ownership management functions are externally reachable (transferOwnership, renounceOwnership) depending on owner-manager-contracts implementation.
No upgrade mechanism. Write functions are externally callable; security relies on vaults calling with msg.sender as vault key.
No upgrade mechanism. Inherits OZ Multicall (external aggregate call surface).
